title: anchusa calcarea subsp. losadae
intro: Anchusa calcarea subsp. losadae, also known as Losada's alkanna, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family Boraginaceae. It is primarily found in the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal problems, and has been shown to have antioxidant and antiseptic properties, which can help to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, Losada's alkanna is often used in infusions, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
